title: Colour-wash backgrounds for calligraphy
materials: masking tape (for taping paper to a board while it dries)
cartridge-paper-220 as alternative if hot-press not available
sources: SYNTHESISED — watercolour-under-calligraphy technique. Draws on watercolour technique principles documented in general PD art instruction (general technique, not copyright specific).
notes: Laying a wet-in-wet or variegated watercolour wash onto hot-press paper, drying it flat, then writing calligraphy on top once completely dry. The wash must be fully dry before any ink touches it. Covers: taping the paper to a board before wetting (prevents buckling), loading two or three colours wet-in-wet so they blend at the edges, tilting the board to encourage flow, drying flat (never with a heat gun — blisters the surface), checking dryness (press the back; if cool, still damp), the ink test on a scrap piece (some wash papers bleed ink differently than plain). What works well: Foundational and Italic; what doesn't: Copperplate (the pointed nib catches the paper tooth). Differentiate from backgrounds that use gum ammoniac or size.
